glazes.name: Black
glazes.description: CR-1 is a glossy black glaze that crawls into raised shapes of varying sizes and forms, creating unpredictable, natural patterns on the surface. The result adds unique texture and visual interest to each piece. Increased coats create larger glaze dots, while thinner coats produce a finer bead effect. More cracks develop with more coats.

The result adds unique texture and visual interest to each piece. Increased coats create larger glaze dots, while thinner coats produce a finer bead effect. More cracks develop with more coats. Fire to cone 5-6 for best results.

The result features a variety of crackle patterns and showcases an ancient glaze's beauty on AMACO No.11 A-Mix White Stoneware.

Fire to cone 5-6 for best results.

glazes.name: Dark Blue

glazes.name: Speckled Teal
glazes.description: CR-22 is a glossy teal glaze with black specks that develops unpredictable, natural patterns when fired, adding unique texture and visual interest to each piece. The surface marked by crawling into raised shapes of varying sizes forms an ancient glaze-like appearance. Opaque and crawly, it features hairline cracks as it dries.
glazes.originalDescription:
CR-22 is a glossy teal glaze with black specks that, when fired, crawls into raised shapes of varying sizes and forms. The result is a surface marked by unpredictable, natural patterns that add unique texture and visual interest to each piece. The featured chip image of CR-22 is applied on AMACO No.11 A-Mix White Stoneware.
Apply 1-4 even coats of glaze to cone 04 bisque. Allow each coat to dry almost fully, but before any significant cracking occurs. Crawl glazes will develop hairline cracks as they dry; handle with care. For added durability before firing, layer over an AMACO Celadon glaze or underglaze.
More coats create larger glaze dots, while thinner coats produce a finer bead effect. Increased coats will produce more cracks. Fire to cone 5-6 for best results.

glazes.originalDescription: Bright Yellow is an opaque, lemon colored glaze with a consistent, reliable result. Inspired by classic Fiesta tableware ceramics, this glaze has a glossy finish and can be used as a liner or base for layering other glazes. Suitable for firing cone 5 and up, it has yielded great results in wood, soda, and salt firings at temperatures up to cone 12.

glazes.name: Scarlet Red
glazes.description: Scarlet Red is an opaque, crimson glaze that provides a consistent, reliable result suitable for classrooms. This glaze is food-safe and can be applied by spray. It works well as both a liner and a base for layering other glazes. Scarlet Red is appropriate for firing at cone 5 and above.
glazes.originalDescription:
Scarlet Red is an opaque, crimson glaze that has a consistent, reliable result that is great for classrooms.
Originally part of the Celebration line, a series inspired by the classic Fiesta tableware ceramics, Scarlet Red is food-safe, safe for spray application, and great as a liner or even a base for layering other glazes. Suitable for firing cone 5 and up.
Several artists have experimented with the Celebration glazes in wood, soda, and salt at temperatures up to cone 12, which yielded great results!
Make sure you always test when exploring alternative firings for any of AMACO's glazes.

glazes.name: Orangerie
glazes.description: Orangerie is an opaque, yellow-orange glaze with a consistent result. Suitable for firing from cone 5 and up, it can be used as a liner or base for layering other glazes. It is food-safe and suitable for spray application. While primarily designed for traditional firings, artists have experimented successfully with wood, soda, and salt firings at higher temperatures.
glazes.originalDescription:

glazes.name: Clementine
glazes.description: Clementine is an opaque, vibrant orange glaze that offers a consistent, reliable result suitable for classrooms. This glaze is food-safe and can be applied by spray or as a liner or base for layering other glazes. It works well for firing at cone 5 and above.
glazes.originalDescription:

glazes.name: Lilac
glazes.description: Lilac is an opaque, light purple glaze with pooling and breaking effects on the surface, inspired by classic Fiesta tableware ceramics. Suitable for firing cone 5 and up, it can be used as a liner or base for layering other glazes. The color may lose intensity at higher temperatures.
glazes.originalDescription: Lilac is an opaque, light purple glaze with a consistent, reliable result. It has been inspired by classic Fiesta tableware ceramics and features pooling and breaking effects on the surface. Suitable for firing cone 5 and up, it can be used as a liner or base for layering other glazes. The color may lose intensity at higher temperatures.

glazes.name: Amethyst
glazes.description: Amethyst is an opaque, dark purple glaze with a rich, ancient appearance inspired by classic Fiesta tableware ceramics. It has a consistent, reliable result suitable for classrooms and can be used as a liner or base for layering other glazes. This glaze is food-safe and can achieve great results in wood, soda, and salt firings up to cone 12.
glazes.originalDescription: Amethyst is an opaque, dark purple glaze with a consistent, reliable result suitable for classrooms. It may lighten in color at higher firing temperatures. Originally part of the Celebration line, inspired by classic Fiesta tableware ceramics, Amethyst has a rich, ancient glaze appearance and is food-safe. It can be used as a liner or base for layering other glazes and is suitable for firing up to cone 5. Several artists have achieved great results using it in wood, soda, and salt firings up to cone 12.

glazes.name: Turquoise
glazes.description: Turquoise is an intense, satin teal glaze producing attractive effects at mid-range temperatures and can be compared to ancient glazes. It is opaque and can achieve striking visual results with just one coat.
glazes.originalDescription: Turquoise is an intense, satin teal glaze that produces attractive high-fire effects at mid-range temperatures. Originally part of the Sahara line.

glazes.name: Coral
glazes.description: Coral is a vibrant persimmon-colored glaze producing intense color and attractive high-fire effects.
glazes.originalDescription: Coral is a glossy, vibrant persimmon-colored glaze that produces intense color and attractive high-fire effects at mid-range temperatures.

glazes.name: Red Gloss
glazes.description: Red Gloss is a glossy deep red glaze that produces intense color and attractive high-fire effects.
glazes.originalDescription: Red Gloss is a glossy deep red glaze that produces intense color and attractive high-fire effects at mid range temperatures.

glazes.name: Snow Drift
glazes.description: KI -11 Snow Drift is a diffused, milky white glaze. The Kiln Ice glazes are modeled after traditional Guan or Snowflake glazes and feature a delicate network of fine hairline cracks (known as crazing). They are translucent but become slightly opaque where the cracks form, creating a beautiful, multi-layered visual texture.
glazes.originalDescription: KI -11 Snow Drift is a diffused, milky white glaze. The Kiln Ice glazes are modeled after traditional Guan or Snowflake glazes and feature a delicate network of fine hairline cracks (known as crazing). They are translucent but become slightly opaque where the cracks form, creating a beautiful, multi-layered visual texture. These glazes can have differences in application and firing technique affecting end results. Thumbnail images are shown on AMACO No.32 Dark Chocolate clay, fired to cone 5.

glazes.name: Arctic Blush
glazes.description: KI-18 Arctic Blush is a pastel pink glaze with a delicate, milky quality. The Kiln Ice glazes feature a delicate network of fine hairline cracks (crazing), creating a beautiful, multi-layered visual texture that becomes slightly opaque where the cracks form.
glazes.originalDescription: KI-18 Arctic Blush is a pastel pink glaze with a delicate, milky quality. The Kiln Ice glazes are modeled after traditional Guan or Snowflake glazes and feature a delicate network of fine hairline cracks (known as crazing). They are translucent but become slightly opaque where the cracks form, creating a beautiful, multi-layered visual texture.

glazes.name: Winter Glass
glazes.description: KI-21 Winter Glass is a pastel, frosty blue featuring a delicate network of fine hairline cracks, creating a beautiful, multi-layered visual texture that becomes slightly opaque where the cracks form.
glazes.originalDescription: KI-21 Winter Glass is a pastel, frosty blue. The glazes feature a delicate network of fine hairline cracks, creating a beautiful, multi-layered visual texture that becomes slightly opaque where the cracks form.

glazes.name: Glacial Lake
glazes.description: KI-27 Glacial Lake is a vibrant blue-green hue. The glazes feature a delicate network of fine hairline cracks (crazing). They are translucent but become slightly opaque where the cracks form, creating a beautiful, multi-layered visual texture.
glazes.originalDescription: KI-27 Glacial Lake is a vibrant, blue-green hue. The Kiln Ice glazes feature a delicate network of fine hairline cracks (known as crazing). They are translucent but become slightly opaque where the cracks form, creating a beautiful, multi-layered visual texture. Thumbnail images are shown on AMACO No.32 Dark Chocolate clay, fired to cone 5.

glazes.name: Frozen Fern
glazes.description: Frozen Fern is a muddled yellow-green hue. The glaze features a delicate network of fine hairline cracks, creating a beautiful, multi-layered visual texture with translucency that becomes slightly opaque in certain areas.
glazes.originalDescription: Frozen Fern is a muddled yellow-green hue. The glaze features a delicate network of fine hairline cracks (known as crazing). It is translucent but becomes slightly opaque where the cracks form, creating a beautiful, multi-layered visual texture.

glazes.name: Honey Crystal
glazes.description: KI-68 Honey Crystal is a warm, golden, and buttery yellow color. These glazes feature a delicate network of fine hairline cracks (crazing) and are translucent but become slightly opaque where the cracks form, creating a beautiful, multi-layered visual texture.
glazes.originalDescription: KI-68 Honey Crystal is a warm, golden, and buttery yellow color. The Kiln Ice glazes are modeled after traditional Guan or Snowflake glazes. They feature a delicate network of fine hairline cracks (known as crazing). These glazes are translucent but become slightly opaque where the cracks form, creating a beautiful, multi-layered visual texture.

glazes.name: Saturation Metallic
glazes.description: Satin metallic black glaze that resembles steel and other metals. Great as an accent glaze.
glazes.originalDescription: Satin metallic black glaze that resembles steel and other metals. Great as an accent glaze. NO_DESCRIPTION

glazes.name: Saturation Gold
glazes.description: Saturation Gold has a smooth, satin/matte finish that is a dark gold similar to bronze when fired. Perfect for sculptural work and industrial ware, this glaze could be a unique accent for any work of art.
glazes.originalDescription:
Saturation Gold has a smooth, satin/matte finish that is a dark gold similar to bronze when fired. Perfect for sculptural work and industrial ware, this glaze could be a unique accent for any work of art. Saturation Gold requires a heavy application to develop its gold metallic finish. 
Due to the powdered nature of the materials involved with the dry-mix dipping buckets of this product, their respective health information and labels differ from the brushing glazes.

glazes.name: Palladium
glazes.description: This unique metallic glaze offers a mirrorlike finish when fired, enhancing any work with beautiful, shimmering accents. It requires three coats to achieve the smooth mirror finish. The fluid nature of the glaze necessitates careful firing to avoid overfiring. Ideal for low porosity bodies, it excels as an accent or when layered with other AMACO glazes, adding depth and visual interest.
glazes.originalDescription:
This unique metallic glaze has a mirrorlike finish when fired and adds beautiful, shimmering accents to any work. Because it is not food safe, this glaze is perfect for sculptural work that calls for reflective, glassy surfaces. Palladium requires 3 coats to develop the smooth mirror finish. This glaze is fluid so be careful not to overfire. This glaze likes low porosity bodies. It is also great as an accent glaze or for layering with other AMACO glazes.
*Note that all dry dipping glazes can be difficult to layer. Dipping glazes behave differently from brushing and they require thorough testing pri

glazes.name: Vintage Gold
glazes.description: Vintage Gold is an opaque metallic glaze developing small triangular gold crystals with dark haloes, creating a glittering patina effect. Not breaking over texture, this dramatic glaze is perfect for sculptural or non-functional work.
glazes.originalDescription: Vintage Gold is a glossy metallic glaze which develops small triangular gold crystals with dark haloes like a glittering patina. This opaque glaze does not break over texture. A dramatic glaze for sculptural or non-functional work. Glaze may run.

glazes.name: June Bug
glazes.description: June Bug is a glossy, dark green metallic glaze that develops small triangular iridescent crystals, similar to the wings of a beetle. This opaque glaze does not break over texture.
glazes.originalDescription: June Bug is a glossy, dark green metallic glaze that develops small triangular iridescent crystals, like the wings of a beetle. This opaque glaze does not break over texture.

glazes.name: Blue Spark
glazes.description: Royal blue glaze with small triangular dark gray metallic crystals, resembling lapis lazuli, and an opaque surface that does not break over texture.
glazes.originalDescription: Blue Spark is a glossy, royal blue glaze which develops small triangular dark gray metallic crystals that make this glaze look like lapis lazuli. This opaque glaze does not break over texture.

glazes.name: Blue Midnight
glazes.description: Blue Midnight creates glossy black with blue highlights where thick. Texture accentuates the effect.
glazes.originalDescription: Blue Midnight is a glossy black glaze that breaks into blue highlights where thick. This glaze benefits from heavy application, creating more separation from the base glaze to the float. Texture accentuates this effect.

glazes.name: Serpentine Green
glazes.description: Serpentine Green is a semi-matte soft green glaze that breaks light blue over texture. It is opaque and translucent, creating an ancient glaze effect.
glazes.originalDescription: PC-13 Serpentine Green is a semi-matte soft green glaze that breaks light blue over texture. Opaque and translucent, it creates an ancient glaze effect.

glazes.name: Lapis Flow
glazes.description: Soft blue to purple with breaking green over texture. Opaque and glossy.
glazes.originalDescription: Lapis Flow is a flowy glaze, varying in color from soft blue to purple and breaking green over texture. Opaque and glossy.

glazes.name: Satin Oribe
glazes.description: Satin Oribe adds a wonderful blue-green burnished look to your wares. Over texture, the dark green pools and light grey breaks bring an extra level of depth to this opaque glaze.
glazes.originalDescription: A modern take on a traditional Japanese oribe glaze, Satin Oribe adds a wonderful blue-green burnished look to your wares. Over texture, the dark green pools and light grey breaks bring an extra level of depth to this opaque glaze. Due to the powdered nature of the materials involved with the dry-mix dipping buckets of this product, their respective health information and labels differ from the brushing glazes.

glazes.name: Purple Crystal
glazes.description: Matte violet with glossier deep indigo tones and purple crystal floats.
glazes.originalDescription: Purple Crystal is a matte violet glaze for Cone 5 that becomes glossy deep indigo with purple crystal floats at Cone 6.

glazes.name: Honey Flux
glazes.description: Ancient glazes shift from opaque honey and cream to a beautiful fluid white with honey flecks.
glazes.originalDescription: Opaque honey and cream at Cone 5 that shifts to a beautiful fluid white with honey flecks at Cone 6.

glazes.name: Tuscan Blue
glazes.description: Tuscan Blue is a deep blue, semi-matte glaze that breaks light blue to moss green over texture. It is opaque and translucent, creating an ancient glaze effect.
glazes.originalDescription: Tuscan Blue is a deep blue, semi-matte glaze that breaks light blue to moss green over texture. Opaque and translucent, it creates an ancient glaze effect.

glazes.name: Moon River
glazes.description: PC-19 Moon River is a dreamy, flowy glaze shifting in color from a soft periwinkle to a milky blue. Opaque and glossy, it may create pooling and breaking effects. Similar to ancient glazes, this glaze offers a timeless appearance.

glazes.name: Blue Rutile
glazes.description: Blue Rutile is a flowing light blue glaze that breaks to brown where thinner, always interesting and opaque.
glazes.originalDescription: Blue Rutile is a flowing light blue glaze that breaks brown where thinner and is always interesting and opaque.

glazes.name: Arctic Blue
glazes.description: Arctic Blue is a beautiful, opaque light blue with a smooth, consistent surface that breaks tan over texture.
glazes.originalDescription: Arctic Blue is a beautiful, opaque light blue with a smooth, consistent surface and breaks tan over texture.

glazes.name: Blue Stone
glazes.description: Blue Stone features a smooth blue-green matte color with varying shades of green transitioning into dark blues on textured surfaces. The glaze appears opaque with an ancient glaze aesthetic.
glazes.originalDescription: Blue Stone carries a softness visible in its smooth blue-green matte color, with different shades of green feathering into dark blues when applied over texture wares. This glaze is opaque and has an ancient glaze appearance.

glazes.name: Indigo Float
glazes.description: Indigo Float is an intensely blue glaze with a lighter blue surface float. This rich cobalt glaze breaks deep blue where thinner, creating an opaque appearance reminiscent of ancient glazes.
glazes.originalDescription: Indigo Float is an intensely blue glaze with a lighter blue surface float. This rich cobalt glaze breaks deep blue where thinner. It has an opaque appearance and is reminiscent of ancient glazes.

glazes.name: Sapphire Float
glazes.description: Sapphire Float is a glossy, denim-blue glaze with wispy white rutile float that breaks green-gray over texture, forming beautiful naturally flowing patterns.
glazes.originalDescription: Sapphire Float is a glossy, denim-blue glaze with wispy white rutile float that breaks green-gray over texture. The rutile float dramatically flows with texture, forming beautiful naturally flowing patterns.

glazes.name: Textured Turquoise
glazes.description: Subtle turquoise gloss, green-beige where thin, but beautiful aqua where thick. Fluid glaze.
glazes.originalDescription: A subtle turquoise gloss, green-beige where thin, but a beautiful aqua where thick. Great for textured ware and layering with other Potter's Choice glazes. It is a fluid glaze.

glazes.name: Blue Lagoon
glazes.description: Cool tones of Blue Lagoon break milky green and pool into light blue shallows. A fluid glaze with interesting surface variations similar to tides in a blue lagoon.
glazes.originalDescription: Cool tones of Blue Lagoon break milky green and pool into light blue shallows. A fluid glaze with interesting surface variations similar to tides in a blue lagoon. NO_DESCRIPTION

glazes.name: Tourmaline
glazes.description: Tourmaline is an intense, opaque blue-green glaze that forms a smooth, strong surface with a crystalline visual effect, comparing artistically to ancient glazes.
glazes.originalDescription: Tourmaline is an intense, opaque blue-green glaze that forms a smooth, strong surface. It has a crystalline visual effect and compares artistically to ancient glazes.

glazes.name: Frosted Turquoise
glazes.description: Frosted Turquoise is an opaque gray-blue glaze that appears to crackle over deep blue, with a smooth, satiny surface. The glaze benefits from heavy application to achieve its color breaking effect.
glazes.originalDescription: Frosted Turquoise is an opaque gray-blue glaze that appears to crackle over deep blue, with a smooth, satiny surface. This glaze benefits from heavy application to achieve its color breaking effect. The glaze color can vary due to iron fuming from nearby pots in firing.

glazes.name: Deep Olive Speckle
glazes.description: Rich, translucent, glossy green glaze with darker green speckles, opaque appearance with pooling and breaking effects, reminiscent of ancient glazes.
glazes.originalDescription: Deep Olive Speckle is a rich, translucent, glossy green glaze with darker green speckles. It has an opaque appearance with pooling and breaking effects, reminiscent of ancient glazes.

glazes.name: Temmoku
glazes.description: Temmoku is a textured, satin-matte dark brown/tan speckled glaze that looks great layered beneath some of the more fluid Potter's Choice glazes. With its consistent surface and reliable results, this glaze makes a fantastic liner for cups, pots, bowls, and other ware.
glazes.originalDescription: Temmoku is a textured, satin-matte dark brown/tan speckled glaze that looks great layered beneath some of the more fluid Potter's Choice glazes. With its consistent surface and reliable results, this glaze makes a fantastic liner for cups, pots, bowls, and other ware.

glazes.name: Oatmeal
glazes.description: Oatmeal is a toasty, light beige glaze that is relatively translucent and breaks clear over textured surfaces. It works well for layering with other Potters Choice glazes.
glazes.originalDescription: Oatmeal is a toasty, light beige glaze that is relatively translucent and breaks clear over textured surfaces. This glaze is great for layering with other Potters Choice glazes. Try layered over PC-33 Iron Lustre!

glazes.name: Albany Slip Brown
glazes.description:
Like the traditional potter's glaze that inspired it, Albany Slip Brown is fluid and butter colored where thick and russet where thin. Great layered over or under other Potter's Choice glazes.
 WARNING:

glazes.name: Iron Lustre
glazes.description: Brown where thin with blue-gray floats where thick. Opaque, crystalline appearance and creates ancient glaze effects.
glazes.originalDescription: Iron Lustre is brown where thin with blue-gray floats where thick. It is beautiful on texture and layers well with other Potter's Choice glazes. This glaze has an opaque, crystalline appearance and can create ancient glaze effects.

glazes.name: Light Sepia
glazes.description: Light Sepia is an opalescent gray glaze that features strong variations on textured ware, breaking golden beige where thin and lending itself well to layering with other Potters Choice Glazes.
glazes.originalDescription: Light Sepia is an opalescent grey glaze featuring strong variations on textured ware. It breaks golden beige where thin and lends itself well to layering with other Potters Choice Glazes.

glazes.name: Ironstone
glazes.description: Ironstone is a glossy, dark glaze that is a deep chocolatey brown where thin and beige-bronze where thicker. It is opaque and mixes well with other Potter's Choice Glazes. The surface has an ancient glaze quality.
glazes.originalDescription: Ironstone is a glossy, dark glaze that is a deep chocolatey brown where thin with a beige-bronze float where thicker. It is opaque and mixes well with other Potter's Choice Glazes. The surface has an ancient glaze quality.

glazes.name: Iron Yellow
glazes.description: Glossy, soft yellow glaze with flecks of iron, relatively opaque and breaks clear over texture.
glazes.originalDescription: Iron Yellow is a glossy, soft yellow glaze with flecks of iron, that is relatively opaque and breaks clear over texture.

glazes.name: True Celadon
glazes.description: True Celadon is a delicate light green glaze that reproduces the effects of traditional Asian celadons. This glaze looks striking over any textured ware, offering great color response when layered with other Potter's Choice glazes. The surface finish is translucent and the glaze can create pooling and breaking effects.
glazes.originalDescription: True Celadon is a delicate light green glaze that reproduces the effects of traditional Asian celadons. This glaze looks striking over any textured ware and has great color response when layered with our other Potter's Choice glazes. Due to the powdered nature of the materials involved with the dry-mix dipping buckets of this product, their respective health information and labels differ from the brushing glazes. *Note that all dry dipping glazes can be difficult to layer. Dipping glazes behave differently from brushing and they require thorough testing prior to layering full piece.

glazes.name: Vert Lustre
glazes.description: Vert Lustre is a beautiful flowing green glaze with a metallic sheen like ancient Persian Lusterware, creating an opaque, glossy surface finish.
glazes.originalDescription: Vert Lustre is a beautiful flowing green glaze with a metallic sheen like ancient Persian Lusterware. This glaze creates an opaque, glossy surface finish.

glazes.name: Seaweed
glazes.description: Seaweed is a deep green glaze that layers beautifully with other Potter's Choice glazes. It breaks into a brownish pattern over texture and becomes more fluid with thickness. Apply in multiple coats for full opacity.
glazes.originalDescription: Seaweed is a fluid, deep green glaze that layers beautifully with a variety of Potter's Choice glazes. This glaze breaks brownish over texture and becomes more fluid with thickness. Pay attention to application thickness so the glaze does not flow onto kiln shelves during firing.

glazes.name: Toasted Sage
glazes.description: Ancient glaze appearance, opaque warm gray with subtle frothy tones of pink and green.
glazes.originalDescription: Toasted Sage is an opaque, warm gray glaze with subtle, frothy tones of pink and green. This glaze has an ancient glaze appearance.

glazes.name: Sage
glazes.description: Sage is an opaque, warm gray green glaze with a glossy surface finish that can create pooling effects. Similar in appearance to ancient glazes, it works well as a top coat with other Potter's Choice glazes and enhances the visual effects of pottery pieces.
glazes.originalDescription: Sage is an opaque, warm gray green glaze. It has a glossy surface finish and can create pooling effects. Similar in appearance to ancient glazes, it works well as a top coat with other Potter's Choice glazes. The glaze is translucent and can enhance the visual effects of pottery pieces.

glazes.name: Dark Green
glazes.description: Dark green glaze showcases textured and carved ware with an elegant, translucent finish.
glazes.originalDescription: Dark green is a fluid, translucent emerald glaze that shows off textured and carved ware.

glazes.name: Lustrous Jade
glazes.description: Lustrous jade is a glossy, opaque glaze that boasts a soft, even green finish like the gemstone it is named for. This glaze layers well with our other Potter's Choice glazes.
glazes.originalDescription: Lustrous jade is a glossy, opaque glaze that boasts a soft, even green finish like the gemstone it is named for. This glaze layers well with our other Potter's Choice glazes. Due to the powdered nature of the materials involved with the dry-mix dipping buckets of this product, their respective health information and labels differ from the brushing glazes. *Note that all dry dipping glazes can be difficult to layer. Dipping glazes behave differently from brushing and they require thorough testing prior to layering full piece.

glazes.name: Emerald Falls
glazes.description: Emerald Falls is a beautiful glossy green glaze that breaks lighter and pools into a mauve grey-green. When heavier coats are applied, it creates interesting surface variations echoing mossy patches found in rolling emerald landscapes. This glaze is opaque and allows the glaze to float up, creating unique textures.
glazes.originalDescription: Emerald Falls is a beautiful glossy green glaze that breaks lighter and pools into a mauve grey-green. When heavier coats are applied this allows the glaze to float up and create interesting surface variations echoing mossy patches found in rolling emerald landscapes. Due to the powdered nature of the materials involved with the dry-mix dipping buckets of this product, their respective health information and labels differ from the brushing glazes. *Note that all dry dipping glazes can be difficult to layer. Dipping glazes behave differently from brushing and they require thorough testing prior to layering full piece.

glazes.name: Art Deco Green
glazes.description: Art Deco Green is a glossy, light blue-green glaze with metallic spots floating to the surface, reminiscent of a patinated copper roof.
glazes.originalDescription: Art Deco Green is a glossy, light blue-green glaze that features metallic spots floating to the surface, reminiscent of a patinated copper roof.

glazes.name: Frosted Melon
glazes.description: Frosted Melon has a massive, beige float that cracks to reveal green underneath while maintaining a smooth, satin surface. The subtle color variation due to iron fuming makes it beautiful on both smooth and textured ware. This glaze is opaque and can create interesting visual effects.
glazes.originalDescription: Frosted Melon is a very unique glaze with a massive, beige float that cracks to reveal green underneath while maintaining a smooth, satin surface. The subtle color variation due to iron fuming makes it beautiful on both smooth and textured ware. This glaze is opaque and can create interesting visual effects.

glazes.name: Deep Sienna Speckle
glazes.description: Deep Sienna Speckle is a warm, rich russet glaze that is translucent with dark speckles resembling the surface of reduction stoneware glazes.
glazes.originalDescription: Deep Sienna Speckle is a warm, rich russet glaze that is translucent with dark speckles resembling the surface of reduction stoneware glazes. This glaze contains black speckles.

glazes.name: Ancient Jasper
glazes.description: Ancient Jasper is a fluid, opaque glaze that transitions from soft black to olive green to rust red as it is applied thicker. This glaze has an active, varied surface by itself and layers with our other Potter's Choice glazes with striking results.
glazes.originalDescription:
Ancient Jasper is a fluid, opaque glaze that transitions from soft black to olive green to rust red as it is applied thicker. This glaze has an active, varied surface by itself and layers with our other Potter's Choice glazes with striking results.
Ancient Jasper benefits from a heavier application. This glaze will produce reds on all clay bodies, although iron-bearing clay bodies will produce the widest range of colors. No special cooling cycles or soaks are needed. Soaking can actually diminish the reds. 
Due to the powdered nature of the materials involved with the dry-mix dipping buckets of this product, their respective health information and labels differ from the brushing glazes.
*Note that all dry dipping glazes can be difficult to layer. Dipping glazes behave differently

glazes.name: Chun Plum
glazes.description: Chun Plum is a pink-red translucent glaze that breaks light tan over textures. This glaze imitates the copper reds of ancient Asian ceramics, providing hints of pink and red in oxidation firings.
glazes.originalDescription: Chun Plum is a pink-red translucent glaze that breaks light tan over textures. This glaze imitates the copper reds of Asia but with a reliable color response in oxidation firings. Layer over other Potters Choice glazes for a warmer tone and hints of pink and red. Due to the powdered nature of the materials involved with the dry-mix dipping buckets of this product, their respective health information and labels differ from the brushing glazes. *Note that all dry dipping glazes can be difficult to layer. Dipping glazes behave differently from brushing and they require thorough testing prior to layering full piece.

glazes.name: Ancient Copper
glazes.description: Ancient Copper is an iridescent iron red glaze that breaks dark brown over texture and pools dark yellow-green, giving it a weathered appearance. It has a more glossy appearance with metallic floats at cone 6.
glazes.originalDescription: Ancient Copper is an iridescent iron red glaze that breaks dark brown over texture and pools dark yellow-green, giving it a weathered appearance. It has a satin finish at cone 5 and a more glossy appearance with metallic floats at cone 6.

glazes.name: Smokey Merlot
glazes.description: Smokey Merlot is a warm, dark purple glaze with a soft float of lilac. It creates beautiful visual effects like pooling and breaking on both smooth and textured surfaces and has an opaque appearance.
glazes.originalDescription: Smokey Merlot is a warm, dark purple glaze with a soft float of lilac. It appears stunning on smooth and textured surfaces, creating beautiful visual effects like pooling and breaking. The glaze has an opaque appearance.

glazes.name: Deep Firebrick
glazes.description: Deep Firebrick is an opaque, brick red glaze that becomes translucent where thin. This bold, dark red glaze has an ancient glaze appearance and contains black specks.
glazes.originalDescription: Deep Firebrick is an opaque, brick red glaze that breaks translucent where thin. This bold, dark red glaze has an ancient glaze appearance and contains black specks.

glazes.name: Textured Amber
glazes.description: Semi-matte brown with varied, translucent surface and opaque tan speckles.
glazes.originalDescription: Textured Amber is a semi-matte brown glaze with a varied, translucent surface and opaque tan speckles.

glazes.name: Textured Amber Brown
glazes.description: Textured Amber Brown is a glossy brown glaze that breaks lighter where thinner like a traditional temmoku glaze. This glaze layers well with other Potter's Choice glazes and produces fluid, beautiful effects.
glazes.originalDescription: Textured Amber Brown is a glossy brown glaze that breaks lighter where thinner like a traditional temmoku glaze. This glaze layers well with our other Potter's Choice glazes and produces fluid, beautiful effects.

glazes.name: Cosmic Tea Dust
glazes.description: Cosmic Tea Dust is a sparkly, dark brown glaze that breaks into a light yellow-brown over texture, shifting subtly from silver to copper with an iridescent quality.
glazes.originalDescription: Cosmic Tea Dust is a sparkly, dark brown glaze that breaks into a light yellow-brown over texture. The sparkle shifts subtly from silver to copper, giving the glaze an iridescent quality.

glazes.name: Aventurine
glazes.description: Aventurine is an amber glaze with crystal flecks that break golden over texture and pool a darker shade in recessed areas. This glaze has an opaque surface finish.
glazes.originalDescription: Aventurine is an amber glaze with crystal flecks, that breaks golden over texture and pools a darker shade in recessed areas. This glaze has an opaque surface finish.

glazes.name: Black Aventurine
glazes.description: Black Aventurine develops a metallic sparkle and can grow crystals if slowly cooled.
glazes.originalDescription: Black Aventurine develops a metallic sparkle and can grow crystals if slow cooled.

glazes.name: Cosmic Oilspot
glazes.description: Cosmic Oil Spot is a dark glaze with shimmery flecks creating streams and spots of color, reminiscent of traditional hare's fur glazes. The surface has an opaque appearance with visual effects similar to ancient glazes.
glazes.originalDescription: Cosmic Oil Spot is a dark glaze with shimmery flecks that create streams and spots of color similar to traditional hare's fur glazes. The surface finish has an opaque appearance, with visual effects reminiscent of ancient glazes.

glazes.name: River Rock
glazes.description: Sandy, dark brown tones feather into a beautiful ebony before breaking into a rich amber when applied over texture. Subtle green-grey honeycomb shapes appear underneath the amber breaks on vertical surfaces. The glossy glaze has an opaque appearance with visual effects of pooling and breaking.
glazes.originalDescription: The sandy, dark brown tones of River Rock feather into a beautiful ebony before breaking into a rich amber when applied over texture. Subtle green-grey honeycomb shapes appear underneath the amber breaks when this glossy glaze is applied on vertical surfaces. Due to the powdered nature of the materials involved with the dry-mix dipping buckets of this product, their respective health information and labels differ from the brushing glazes.

glazes.name: Golden Honey
glazes.description: Translucent warm yellow, Golden Honey breaks lighter on texture, a richer color where thick. Opaque and glossy, like whipped honey.

glazes.name: Cosmic Gold Dust
glazes.description: Cosmic Gold Dust is a sparkly gold glaze with subtle specks of reds and browns in the light. Opaque and glossy, it may show pooling and breaking effects. Comparable to ancient glazes, this glaze can be used over AMACO Celadons, other Potter's Choice and Satin Matte glazes.

glazes.name: Copper Red

glazes.name: Flambe

glazes.name: Fire & Ice
glazes.description: PC-72 Fire & Ice is deep red where thick and icy blue where thin. It features pooling effects, creating an ancient glaze appearance and is opaque. The glaze has a dramatic icy blue-colored break on texture.

glazes.name: Midnight Run

glazes.name: Honeydew

DL-63 Stardust is a dark brown and black glaze with sparkles that shift between silver and copper. This glaze works well as a base or top layer when paired with other DL glazes.
