glazes.name: Speckled Grapel
glazes.description: Speckled Grapel is a heavily pigmented, viscous Stroke & Coat® glaze with blue specks. One coat creates a translucent finish; two to three coats are suggested for full opacity and complete coverage. It fires to a glossy finish without clear glaze. The color slightly lightens upon firing, and the specks bleed.
glazes.originalDescription: Speckled Grapel is a heavily pigmented, viscous Stroke & Coat® glaze with blue specks. One coat will create a translucent finish; subsequent coats add opacity. Two to three coats are suggested for full opacity and all over coverage. It fires to a glossy finish without clear glaze. The color slightly lightens upon firing, and the specks bleed.

glazes.name: Speckled Tuxedo
glazes.description: One coat creates a translucent finish, while additional coats increase opacity. Fire to a glossy finish. Speckled Tuxedo has an opaque, heavily pigmented appearance with white specks.
glazes.originalDescription: Speckled Tuxedo is a heavily pigmented, viscous Stroke & Coat® glaze with white specks that can be used for brush strokes or opaque coverage. One coat will create a translucent finish with subsequent coats adding opacity. Will fire to a gloss finish without clear glaze. Opaque. Color slightly Darkens.

glazes.name: Speckled Cotton Tail
glazes.description: One coat creates a translucent finish, while two to three coats add opacity and cover fully. Speckled Cotton Tail features yellow, orange, and blue specks that blend subtly.
glazes.originalDescription: Speckled Cotton Tail is a heavily pigmented, viscous Stroke & Coat® glaze with yellow, orange, and blue specks that can be used for brush strokes or opaque coverage. One coat will create a translucent finish with subsequent coats adding opacity. Two to three coats are suggested for full opacity and all over coverage. Specks blur and slightly bleed.

glazes.name: Speckled Green Thumb
glazes.description: Speckled Green Thumb is a heavily pigmented, viscous glaze with black specks that creates a translucent finish, becoming more opaque with additional coats.
glazes.originalDescription: Speckled Green Thumb is a heavily pigmented, viscous glaze with black specks that can create a translucent finish with subsequent coats becoming more opaque. Opaque. No color change.

glazes.name: Speckled Sour Apple
glazes.description: Speckled Sour Apple is a heavily pigmented, viscous Stroke & Coat® glaze with blue specks that can be used for brush strokes or opaque coverage. One coat will create a translucent finish with subsequent coats adding opacity. We suggest two to three coats for full opacity and all over coverage. Will fire to a gloss finish without clear glaze. Opaque. No color change.
glazes.originalDescription: Cone 06 oxidation (larger image):Speckled Sour Apple is a heavily pigmented, viscous Stroke & Coat® glaze with blue specks that can be used for brush strokes or opaque coverage. One coat will create a translucent finish with subsequent coats adding opacity. We suggest two to three coats for full opacity and all over coverage. Will fire to a gloss finish without clear glaze.Cone 6 oxidation (smaller image):Opaque. No color change.

glazes.name: Speckled The Blues
glazes.description: Speckled The Blues is an opaque, heavily pigmented glaze with blue specks, creating an ancient glaze effect. It has a glossy finish.
glazes.originalDescription: Speckled The Blues is an opaque, heavily pigmented glaze with blue specks, creating an ancient glaze effect. It has a glossy finish when fired.

glazes.name: Speckled My Blue Heaven
glazes.description: Speckled My Blue Heaven is a heavily pigmented, viscous Stroke & Coat® glaze with blue specks that can be used for brush strokes or opaque coverage. One coat will create a translucent finish with subsequent coats adding opacity. We suggest two to three coats for full opacity and all over coverage. Will fire to a gloss finish without clear glaze.
glazes.originalDescription: Cone 06 oxidation (larger image):Speckled My Blue Heaven is a heavily pigmented, viscous Stroke & Coat® glaze with blue specks that can be used for brush strokes or opaque coverage. One coat will create a translucent finish with subsequent coats adding opacity. We suggest two to three coats for full opacity and all over coverage. Will fire to a gloss finish without clear glaze.Cone 6 oxidation (smaller image):Opaque. Color lightens to medium brown. Specks remain.

glazes.name: Speckled Purple Haze
glazes.description: Speckled Purple Haze is a heavily pigmented, viscous glaze with blue specks that can be used for brush strokes or opaque coverage. One coat will create a translucent finish; subsequent coats add opacity until full opacity is reached. Fires to a glossy finish without the need for clear glaze. Color slightly lightens during firing and specks bleed.
glazes.originalDescription: Speckled Purple Haze is a heavily pigmented, viscous glaze with blue specks that can be used for brush strokes or opaque coverage. One coat will create a translucent finish with subsequent coats adding opacity and full opacity. Will fire to a gloss finish without clear glaze. Color slightly lightens during firing. Specks bleed.

glazes.name: Speckled Vanilla Dip
glazes.description: Speckled Vanilla Glaze is heavily pigmented with orange, brown, and black specks, creating an opaque, glossy finish. The glaze has an ancient glaze appearance with visible pooling and breaking effects.
glazes.originalDescription: Speckled Vanilla Glaze is heavily pigmented with orange, brown, and black specks, creating an opaque, glossy finish. Multiple coats can be applied to achieve full opacity and complete coverage. The glaze has an ancient glaze appearance with visible pooling and breaking effects.

glazes.name: Speckled Silver Lining
glazes.description: Speckled Silver Lining is heavily pigmented with red and black specks, creating an opaque coverage. One coat produces a translucent finish; additional coats increase opacity. Upon firing, the color transforms to medium blue, with specks blurring and bleeding. The glaze results in a glossy finish.
glazes.originalDescription: Speckled Silver Lining is heavily pigmented with red and black specks, creating an opaque coverage. One coat produces a translucent finish; additional coats increase opacity. Fire results in a glossy finish. Color transforms to medium blue upon firing, with specks blurring and bleeding.

glazes.name: Speckled Pink-A-Dot
glazes.description: Speckled Pink-A-Dot creates a translucent finish with subsequent coats adding opacity, suggesting two to three coats for full coverage and opacity. Fires to a glossy finish without clear glaze. Specks bleed.
glazes.originalDescription: Speckled Pink-A-Dot creates a translucent finish with subsequent coats adding opacity, suggesting two to three coats for full coverage and opacity. Fires to a glossy finish without clear glaze. Color slightly lightens at Cone 6 oxidation temperature. Specks bleed.

glazes.name: Speckled Hot Tamale
glazes.description: Speckled Hot Tamale is a heavily pigmented, viscous Stroke & Coat® glaze with white specks that can be used for brush strokes or opaque coverage. One coat will create a translucent finish with subsequent coats adding opacity. We suggest two to three coats for full opacity and all over coverage. Will fire to a gloss finish without clear glaze.
glazes.originalDescription: Cone 06 oxidation (larger image):Speckled Hot Tamale is a heavily pigmented, viscous Stroke & Coat® glaze with white specks that can be used for brush strokes or opaque coverage. One coat will create a translucent finish with subsequent coats adding opacity. We suggest two to three coats for full opacity and all over coverage. Will fire to a gloss finish without clear glaze.Cone 6 oxidation (smaller image):Opaque. Color lightens to medium brown. Specks remain.

glazes.name: Speckled Orange-A-Peel
glazes.description: Speckled Orange-A-Peel creates a translucent finish, with two to three coats recommended for full opacity and complete coverage. It fires to a glossy finish without the need for a clear glaze. The color slightly lightens upon firing, and specks bleed.
glazes.originalDescription: Speckled Orange-A-Peel creates a translucent finish with subsequent coats adding opacity. Two to three coats are suggested for full opacity and complete coverage. Fires to a glossy finish without clear glaze. The color slightly lightens upon firing, and specks bleed.

glazes.name: Speckled Tu Tu Tango
glazes.description: Speckled Tu Tu Tango is a heavily pigmented, viscous Stroke & Coat® glaze with yellow, blue and green specks that can be used for brush strokes or opaque coverage. One coat will create a translucent finish; two to three coats are suggested for full opacity and all over coverage. It will fire to a gloss finish without clear glaze.
glazes.originalDescription: Cone 06 oxidation (larger image):Speckled Tu Tu Tango is a heavily pigmented, viscous Stroke & Coat® glaze with yellow, blue and green specks that can be used for brush strokes or opaque coverage. One coat will create a translucent finish with subsequent coats adding opacity. We suggest two to three coats for full opacity and all over coverage. Will fire to a gloss finish without clear glaze.Cone 6 oxidation (smaller image):Opaque. No color change.

glazes.name: Stoneware Crackle Matte Clear
glazes.description: Crackle Matte Clear is a soft matte, semi-transparent glaze that develops a fine crackle pattern. A minimum of two coats are needed to develop a crackle pattern. To highlight the crackle pattern, after firing, wipe black acrylic or ink over the ware and wipe off, allowing the ink to fill the cracks.
glazes.originalDescription: Cone 6 oxidation (larger image):Crackle Matte Clear is a soft matte, semi-transparent glaze that develops a fine crackle pattern.Cone 10 reduction (smaller image):No Change.TIP:A minimum of two coats are needed to develop a crackle pattern. To highlight the crackle pattern, after firing, wipe black acrylic or ink over the ware and wipe off, allowing the ink to fill the cracks.

glazes.name: Blue Surf
glazes.description: Blue Surf is a variegated cobalt blue glaze that breaks to green.
glazes.originalDescription: Cone 6: Blue Surf is a variegated cobalt blue glaze that breaks green. Cone 10: Color darkens. Where the glaze breaks, it will be green.

glazes.name: Stoned Denim
glazes.description: Stoned Denim is a variegated blue glaze that breaks to brown.
glazes.originalDescription: Stoned Denim is a variegated blue glaze that breaks brown. Where the glaze breaks, it will appear brown.

glazes.name: Black Walnut
glazes.description: Black Walnut is a dark brown soft matte glaze that breaks to a rich warm brown. Rich brown undertones come through. The thinner the application, the lighter brown the color; the thicker the application, the darker the brown color. For a saturated opaque finish, apply 4+ coats. Has a satin finish.
glazes.originalDescription: Black Walnut is a dark brown soft matte glaze that breaks a rich warm brown. Rich brown undertones come through. The thinner the application, the lighter brown the color; the thicker the application, the darker the brown color. For a saturated opaque finish, apply 4+ coats. Has a satin finish. Wood and soda firing results will vary.

glazes.name: Frost Blue
glazes.description: Frost Blue is a mottled blue soft matte glaze that breaks into muted green/gray. It can exhibit semi-gloss finish with variegated blue/light blue and green hues.
glazes.originalDescription: Frost Blue is a mottled blue soft matte glaze that breaks a muted green/gray. It can exhibit semi-gloss finish with variegated blue/light blue and green hues. If you like the results that Robins Egg (SW116) gives you, then you will enjoy this glaze because they are made up of similar things.

glazes.name: Alabaster
glazes.description: Alabaster is a semi-opaque white soft matte glaze that breaks over texture. It has a satin finish and combines well with 2 coats of stroke and coat on top. Wood and soda firing results will vary.
glazes.originalDescription: Cone 6:Alabaster is a semi-opaque white soft matte glaze that breaks over texture. Cone 10:Color darkens to variegated light gray/purple. It has a satin finish. Combos really well with 2 coats of stroke and coat on top. If you like combos with Sea Salt, you will get similar results with Alabaster minus the crystals. Wood and soda firing results will vary.

glazes.name: Dunes
glazes.description: Semi-opaque off-white soft matte glaze that breaks over texture. The lighter the application, the more brown the glaze will appear; the thicker the application, the lighter tan the color.
glazes.originalDescription: Cone 6:Dunes is a semi-opaque off-white soft matte glaze that breaks over texture.Cone 10:Color darkens to variegated gray.TIP:The lighter the application, the more brown the glaze will appear. The thicker the glaze application, the lighter tan the color.

glazes.name: Green Tea
glazes.description: Green Tea is a variegated sage green glaze that breaks over texture, producing a glossy blue finish. Thicker application will produce more crystals. This glaze combos well with other Mayco glazes.
glazes.originalDescription: Green Tea is a variegated sage green glaze that breaks over texture, producing a glossy blue finish. It can change to a variegated pink/blue/green in Cone 10 reduction firing. Thicker application will produce more crystals. This glaze has a glossy finish and combos well with other Mayco glazes.

glazes.name: Capri Blue
glazes.description: Variegated blue/green glaze with breaking effect over texture. Semi-gloss finish. Opaque.
glazes.originalDescription: Variegated blue/green glaze with breaking effect over texture. Semi-gloss finish. Thinner application results in greener glaze, thicker application results in bluer glaze. Opaque finish.

glazes.name: Oyster
glazes.description: Oyster is a variegated cream/brown glaze with gray/purple undertones and breaks brown. An iridescent finish may be achieved by re-firing or if your kiln fires hot. If you like Green Tea and Capri Blue combos, then try Oyster because these three glazes are in the same glaze family.
glazes.originalDescription: Oyster is a variegated cream/brown glaze with gray/purple undertones and breaks brown. Color changes to a variegated brown/cream at Cone 10 reduction, with increased movement. An iridescent finish may be achieved by re-firing or if your kiln fires hot. If you like Green Tea and Capri Blue combos, then try Oyster because these three glazes are in the same glaze family.

glazes.name: Wrought Iron
glazes.description: Wrought Iron creates an intermittent, dappled black glossy-matte finish that is reminiscent of ancient oil-spot glazes. The thicker the application, the more black the glaze; the thinner the application, the more brown the glaze. This glaze has a matte finish with variation of gloss due to its high manganese content and may show bubbling on speckled clay bodies.
glazes.originalDescription: Cone 6: Wrought Iron creates an intermittent, dappled black glossy-matte finish that is reminiscent of ancient oil-spot glazes. The thicker the application, the more black the glaze; the thinner the application, the more brown the glaze. This glaze has a matte finish with variation of gloss due to its high manganese content and may show bubbling on speckled clay bodies. Cone 10: Color changes to a dark metallic gray.

glazes.name: Tiger’s Eye
glazes.description: Tiger’s Eye breaks from a glossy deep brownish-red to frothy amber depending on the application thickness. One to two coats will produce browns and reds; three to five coats will result in more glaze movement and color variation. Thinner applications will produce darker browns and reds; heavier applications will lean towards an opaque, light brown finish. Butted against another glaze, Tiger’s Eye will gently merge with it—SW-104 Black Walnut is a nice complementary glaze to use. Wood and soda firing results will vary.
glazes.originalDescription: Tiger’s Eye breaks from a glossy deep brownish-red to frothy amber depending on the application thickness. One to two coats will produce browns and reds; three to five coats will result in more glaze movement and color variation. Thinner applications will produce darker browns and reds; heavier applications will lean towards an opaque, light brown finish. Butted against another glaze, Tiger’s Eye will gently merge with it – SW-104 Black Walnut is a nice complementary glaze to use. Wood and soda firing results will vary.

glazes.name: Speckled Plum
glazes.description: Speckled Plum produces a reddish brown gloss finish with one to two light coats and evolves to deliver floating maroon-mauve tones with heavier applications of three to four coats. Heavier applications may also yield bands of reddish-mauve specks when used with certain clay bodies. Thinner applications will produce darker browns and reds, while heavier applications will begin to show an opaque, maroon-mauve float.
glazes.originalDescription: Speckled Plum produces a reddish brown gloss finish (one to two light coats) and evolves to deliver floating maroon-mauve tones with heavier applications (three to four coats). Heavier applications may also yield bands of reddish-mauve specks when used with certain clay bodies. Color darkens at Cone 10 reduction firing. Thinner applications will produce darker browns and reds; heavier applications will begin to show an opaque, maroon-mauve float.

glazes.name: Midnight Rain
glazes.description: Midnight Rain is a blue-green breaking gloss glaze with black crystals that create oil-like streaks, darkening the color and increasing movement. The base glaze SW-100 Blue Surf creates a lighter crystal effect when applied with a third coat.
glazes.originalDescription: Midnight Rain is a blue-green breaking gloss glaze that contains a black crystal. As the crystal melts and moves, a black oil-like streak is created in the glaze, darkening the color and increasing movement. The base glaze SW-100 Blue Surf creates a lighter crystal effect when applied with a third coat of SW-115 Midnight Rain.

glazes.name: Robin’s Egg
glazes.description: Robin’s Egg is a greenish-blue matte glaze that contains a sand-colored crystal. A small sandy-brown bloom appears as the crystal melts, creating a mottled, textured finish. The glaze has a semi-gloss surface with variegated blue and light blue hues and crystals.
glazes.originalDescription: Robin’s Egg is a greenish-blue matte glaze that contains a sand-colored crystal. As the crystal melts, a small sandy-brown bloom will appear in the glaze, producing a mottled, textured finish. Semi-gloss finish with variegated blue/light blue and crystals.

glazes.name: Honeycomb
glazes.description: Honeycomb is a variegated brown matte glaze that contains a sandy-brown crystal. As the crystal melts, it creates glossy blooms producing yellow mottling and a textured fired finish. For a lighter crystal effect, apply two coats of SW-103 Sisal with a third coat of SW-117 Honeycomb.
glazes.originalDescription: Honeycomb is a variegated brown matte glaze that contains a sandy-brown crystal. As the crystal melts it creates glossy blooms that can produce yellow mottling in the glaze, producing a textured fired finish. Color darkens at Cone 10. SW-103 Sisal is the base glaze. For a lighter crystal effect, you may try applying two coats of SW-103 Sisal with a third coat of SW-117 Honeycomb.

glazes.name: Sea Salt
glazes.description: Sea Salt is a white matte glaze that contains a sage green crystal. As the crystal melts, green blooms appear in the glaze, producing a foamy, textured fired finish.
glazes.originalDescription: Cone 6:Sea Salt is a white matte glaze that contains a sage green crystal. As the crystal melts, green blooms appear in the glaze, producing a foamy, textured fired finish. Cone 10:Base glaze turns pale lavender/gray with mint green crystals.

glazes.name: Cinnabar
glazes.description: Cinnabar is a rich maroon-red gloss glaze that tends to develop a hazy purple float with heavier application. You'll produce beautiful color variations with Cinnabar.
glazes.originalDescription: Cone 6:Cinnabar is a rich maroon-red gloss glaze that tends to develop a hazy purple float with heavier application. Cone 10:Color darkens. You’ll produce beautiful color variations with Cinnabar.

glazes.name: Northern Woods
glazes.description: Northern Woods is a semi-transparent bluish-green glaze. Lighter applications produce a light translucent green with brown hues, while additional coats deepen the green hues and add a bluish tint. It looks very nice on white stoneware and porcelain. When applied thin, the color appears brown and breaks brown. Apply three heavy coats for the deep green color.
glazes.originalDescription: Northern Woods is a semi-transparent bluish-green glaze. Lighter applications will produce a light translucent green with brown hues. As additional coats are applied, the green hues will deepen and develop a bluish tint. Very nice on white stoneware and porcelain. This glaze breaks brown and when applied thin the color will be brown. Apply three heavy coats to get the deep green color.

glazes.name: Smoke
glazes.description: Smoke is an opaque grey-blue stoneware glaze with soft, subtle variegating hues that change to a variegated sage green with a glossy finish during firing. Brown breaks on raised surfaces on the body.
glazes.originalDescription: Smoke is an opaque grey-blue stoneware glaze with soft, subtle variegating hues. It changes to a variegated sage green with a glossy finish during firing. Brown breaks on raised surfaces on the body.

glazes.name: Maycoshino
glazes.description: Maycoshino is a variegating iron glaze with subtle color changes. In a thin application, it achieves rich, iron browns. Applied thicker, it produces lighter, creamy hues reminiscent of traditional Japanese Shino glazes. This glaze creates a solid reddish brown opaque covering with one coat; two coats result in a cream-tan-buff finish that breaks brown over surface texture; three coats introduce waves of cream-tan color over the reddish brown base.
glazes.originalDescription: Maycoshino is a variegating iron glaze with subtle color changes. In a thin application, the user will achieve rich, iron browns. Applied thicker, the user will get lighter, creamy hues that are reminiscent of traditional Japanese Shino glazes. This glaze produces a solid reddish brown opaque covering with one coat; two coats result in a cream-tan-buff finish that breaks brown over surface texture; three coats introduce waves of cream-tan color over the reddish brown base.

glazes.name: Sapphire
glazes.description: Sapphire is a rich, deep cobalt blue glaze with a glossy, mirrored surface that can give colors ranging from dark blue to bluish-purple, depending on the thickness of application.
glazes.originalDescription: Cone 6:Sapphire is a rich, deep cobalt blue glaze. It has a glossy, mirrored surface which can give colors from dark blue to bluish-purple, depending upon the thickness of application. Cone 10:No Change.

glazes.name: Olivine
glazes.description: Olivine has rusts and several shades of green flow from this mobile gloss glaze. Lighter coats produce muted greens and browns, while 3 coats can produce a beautiful metallic green, almost mirror-like. Color flattens and warm undertones come through.
glazes.originalDescription:
Cone 6 oxidation (larger image): Olivine has rusts and several shades of green flow from this mobile gloss glaze. Lighter coats produce muted greens and browns, while 3 coats can produce a beautiful metallic green, almost mirror-like. Cone 10 reduction (smaller image): Color flattens and warm undertones come through.

NO_DESCRIPTION

glazes.name: Cordovan
glazes.description: Cordovan develops a deep, burgundy red color with a semi-gloss surface when fired to cone 5 or 6. Two coats are recommended for deep red, opaque coverage; light glazes may require a third coat.
glazes.originalDescription: Cordovan develops a deep, burgundy red color with a semi-gloss surface when fired to cone 5 or 6. Color changes to a dark metallic gray with copper/black mottling at cone 10 reduction. Two coats should be plenty to get deep red, opaque coverage; light glazers may need to add a third coat.

glazes.name: Copper Float
glazes.description: Copper Float glaze is a rich and complex midrange glaze with hues varying from reds to coppers to gun metal blacks. The predominant coloration is a dark rusty red. The surface is satin-matte when fired to cone 5 or 6. Finishes flatten at higher temperatures, resulting in warm brown tones with some copper mottling. Thicker coats can produce metallic red hues. Wood and soda firing results will vary.
glazes.originalDescription: Cone 6:Copper Float glaze is a rich and complex midrange glaze with hues varying from reds, to coppers to gun metal blacks. Predominate coloration is a dark rusty red. The surface is satin-matte when fired to cone 5 or 6.Cone 10:Finish flattens. Color changes to a warm brown with some copper mottling.TIP:One coat will produce dark, greenish tones, thicker coats will add greens. Thickest applications will result in metallic red hues.Wood and soda firing results will vary.

glazes.name: Copper Jade
glazes.description: Copper Jade fires to a glossy finish, producing colors that range from brown-greens to creamy tan hues. Color breaks dark and can pool into red tones where thick. This glaze yields complex surfaces through movement and color variation. Color changes to a red/brown with some black/copper mottling.
glazes.originalDescription:
Cone 6: Copper Jade fires to a glossy finish, producing colors that range from brown-greens to creamy tan hues. Color breaks dark and can pool into red tones where thick. This glaze yields complex surfaces through movement and color variation. Cone 10: Color changes to a red/brown with some black/copper mottling.

NO_DESCRIPTION

glazes.name: Birch
glazes.description: Birch is a creamy opaque off-white glaze that breaks to a brown color. A thinner application results in an amber hue, while a thicker application yields more white specks.
glazes.originalDescription: Birch is a creamy opaque off-white glaze that breaks brown. Cream color remains and brown undertones darken at Cone 10 reduction. The thinner the application, the more amber the glaze will be; the thicker the application, the more white floating you will get.

glazes.name: Mirror Black
glazes.description: Mirror Black creates a highly reflective, metallic black gloss finish. On some vertical surfaces and around surface textures, minor matte effects can be seen; all other applications result in the formation of a black, metallic mirror. Color remains. Increased movement.
glazes.originalDescription: Mirror Black creates a highly reflective, metallic black gloss finish. On some vertical surfaces and around surface textures, minor matte effects can be seen; all other applications result in the formation of a black, metallic mirror. Color remains. Increased movement. Not recommended for dinnerware due to surface durability.

glazes.name: Copper Ore
glazes.description: Copper Ore is a highly mobile, reddish brown metallic glaze that creates a dark bronze field with areas of darker coloring and flashes of brighter red metallic. The movement within the glaze produces striking visual textures, turning to red/brown with increased movement.
glazes.originalDescription: Copper Ore is a highly mobile, reddish brown metallic glaze. A dark bronze field breaks with areas of darker coloring and flashes of brighter red metallic. The movement within this glaze produces striking visual textures. Color turns to red/brown with increased movement.

glazes.name: Eggplant
glazes.description: Eggplant is an opaque, gloss purple glaze. Blue-gray colors may develop where the glaze breaks over and around surface textures. Surface textures encourage the glaze to break blue and pool, creating beautiful visual effects when used in combination with other glazes.
glazes.originalDescription: Eggplant is an opaque, gloss purple glaze. Blue-gray colors may develop where the glaze breaks over and around surface textures. At Cone 10, color changes to blue. Surface textures encourage the glaze to break blue and pool, creating beautiful visual effects when used in combination with other glazes.

glazes.name: Wintergreen
glazes.description: Wintergreen is a light-green, semi-opaque matte glaze. Lighter coatings will allow body characteristics to appear through the glaze (especially on dark and speckled clay bodies). Use three coats for deepest opacity. Changes to a transparent pale green gloss with matte crystal growth at higher temperatures.
glazes.originalDescription: Cone 6: Wintergreen is a light-green, semi-opaque matte glaze. Lighter coatings will allow body characteristics to appear through the glaze (especially on dark and speckled clay bodies). Use three coats for deepest opacity. Cone 10: Changes to a transparent pale green gloss with matte crystal growth. Tip: This is a true matte glaze at cone 5, becomes satin at cone 6.

glazes.name: Weathered Blue
glazes.description: Weathered Blue produces a blue to white variegation depending on the application. One coat yields a sky-blue matte finish with little white variegation; additional coats build more white variegation, muting the sky blue. Thinned coats produce a sky blue satin finish, while thick applications increase white variegation. Brushstroke application is visible in the finished results. Coats should be applied on bisque in different directions for best effect. The finish appears opaque, but surface decoration can be seen through it.
glazes.originalDescription: Cone 6:Weathered Blue is designed to produce a blue to white variegation depending on the application. One coat will produce a sky-blue matte finish with little white variegation. Subsequent coats build a white variegation, muting the sky blue.Cone 10:Off-white variation darkens.TIP:Thinned coats produce a sky blue satin finish. Thick applications will increase white variegation. The brushstroke application is visible in the finished results. We recommend applying coats on bisque in different directions (crosshatch). Although the finish appears opaque, surface decoration can be easily seen through the glaze.

glazes.name: Storm Gray
glazes.description: Storm Gray produces a medium gray to white variegation. One coat yields a medium gray matte finish with little white variegation; additional coats build white variegation, lightening the medium gray. Thinned coats result in a medium gray satin finish, while thick applications increase white variegation. Brush stroke application is visible in finished results. Although the finish appears opaque, surface decoration can be easily seen through the glaze.
glazes.originalDescription: Storm Gray produces a medium gray to white variegation. One coat yields a medium gray matte finish with little white variegation; additional coats build white variegation, lightening the medium gray. Off-white variation darkens and gray variation turns blue at Cone 10 reduction temperature. Thinned coats result in a medium gray satin finish, while thick applications increase white variegation. Brush stroke application is visible in finished results. Although the finish appears opaque, surface decoration can be easily seen through the glaze.

glazes.name: Lemon Meringue
glazes.description: Lemon Meringue produces a bright yellow to white variegation. One coat will produce a bright yellow matte finish with little white variegation. Subsequent coats build white variegation, muting the yellow. Thinned coats produce a bright yellow satin finish. Thick applications increase white variegation. Brush stroke application is visible in finished results. Although finish appears opaque, surface decoration can be easily seen through glaze.
glazes.originalDescription: Lemon Meringue produces a bright yellow to white variegation depending on application. One coat will produce a bright yellow matte finish with little white variegation. Subsequent coats build white variegation, muting the yellow. Thinned coats produce a bright yellow satin finish. Thick applications increase white variegation. Brush stroke application is visible in finished results. Although finish appears opaque, surface decoration can be easily seen through glaze.

glazes.name: Black Matte
glazes.description: Black Matte produces a smooth matte surface. Light coats allow clay body characteristics to appear through the glaze (especially on dark and speckled clay bodies). Thicker application will produce a more opaque finish.
glazes.originalDescription: Black Matte produces a smooth matte surface. Light coats allow clay body characteristics to appear through the glaze (especially on dark and speckled clay bodies). Color darkens at cone 10 reduction. A true matte finish with no movement. Thicker application will produce a more opaque finish.

glazes.name: White Matte
glazes.description: White Matte produces a smooth matte surface. Light coats allow clay body characteristics to appear through the glaze, especially on dark and speckled clay bodies. Thick applications of three or more coats result in a more opaque finish.
glazes.originalDescription: Cone 6:White Matte is formulated to produce a smooth matte surface. Light coats will allow clay body characteristics to appear through the glaze (especially on dark and speckled clay bodies).Cone 10:No change.TIP:A true matte finish at cone 5/6 with no movement. Thicker application, three or more coats, will produce a more opaque finish.

glazes.name: Gray Matte
glazes.description: Gray Matte produces a smooth matte surface. Light coats allow clay body characteristics to appear through the glaze, especially on dark and speckled clay bodies. A true matte finish with no movement at cone 5/6. Thicker application will produce a more opaque finish.
glazes.originalDescription: Gray Matte produces a smooth matte surface. Light coats allow clay body characteristics to appear through the glaze (especially on dark and speckled clay bodies). Color changes to a blue/gray at cone 10. A true matte finish with no movement at cone 5/6. Thicker application will produce a more opaque finish.

glazes.name: Abalone
glazes.description: Abalone produces a breaking white and mauve semi-gloss glaze. Light applications result in a more white semi-gloss finish. Three or more coats are needed for a semi-gloss Abalone finish. Clay body characteristics may be visible through this glaze. Color darkens to purple at Cone 10 reduction.
glazes.originalDescription: Cone 6oxidation (larger image):Abalone is formulated to produce a breaking white and mauve semi-gloss glaze. Light applications will produce a more white semi-gloss finish. Use three+ coats to achieve semi-gloss Abalone finish. Clay body characteristics will be visible through this glaze.Cone 10reduction (smaller image):Color darkens to purple.

glazes.name: Aurora Green
glazes.description: Aurora Green produces a mottled aqua gloss with light brown and large black crystals. As the crystals melt, bursts of blue and brown will develop. Color changes to a variegated pink/blue/green. Crystals become less pronounced.
glazes.originalDescription: Aurora Green produces a mottled aqua gloss with light brown and large black crystals. As the crystals melt, bursts of blue and brown will develop. Color changes to a variegated pink/blue/green at Cone 10. Crystals become less pronounced. NO_DESCRIPTION

glazes.name: Moonscape
glazes.description: Moonscape produces a medium gray to white variegation mottled with black crystals. Light coats result in a matte gray finish with minimal white variegation and few crystals. Three or more coats enhance white variegation and crystal presence. At cone 5, it yields a matte sepia finish with little surface breaking.
glazes.originalDescription: Cone 6:Moonscape is formulated to produce a medium gray to white variegation mottled with black crystals. Light coats will produce a matte gray finish with little white variegation and few crystals. Use three or more coats to produce more white variegation and crystals. At cone 5, this glaze will produce a matte sepia finish with little breaking over texture.Cone 10:Off-white variation darkens. Gray variation turns blue. Crystals melt.TIP:SW-137 Storm Gray is the base glaze. For a lighter crystal effect, you may try applying two coats of SW-137 Storm Gray with a third coat of SW-147 Moonscape.

glazes.name: Lime Shower
glazes.description: Lime Shower is formulated to produce a vibrant chartreuse gloss glaze with dark green and white crystals. Light coats will produce a semi-transparent finish in the base glaze with fewer crystals. Use three or more coats to produce an opaque green float with more saturation of crystals. At cone 5, this glaze will produce a matte sepia finish with little breaking over texture. For a lighter crystal effect, you may try applying two coats of SW-253 Green Opal with a third coat of SW-148 Lime Shower.
glazes.originalDescription: Cone 6oxidation (larger image):Lime Shower is formulated to produce a vibrant chartreuse gloss glaze with dark green and white crystals. Light coats will produce a semi-transparent finish in the base glaze with fewer crystals. Use three or more coats to produce an opaque green float with more saturation of crystals. At cone 5, this glaze will produce a matte sepia finish with little breaking over texture.Cone 10reduction (smaller image):Color becomes less vibrant. Crystals melt.TIP:SW-253 Green Opal is the base glaze. For a lighter crystal effect, you may try applying two coats of SW-253 Green Opal with a third coat of SW-148 Lime Shower.

glazes.name: Crackle White
glazes.description: Crackle White is a bright white glossy glaze that develops a fine crackle pattern. It works well on stoneware and porcelain bodies and produces crackle patterns at midrange temperatures. The glaze turns orange-brown and pools white in texture when thick.
glazes.originalDescription: Crackle White is a bright white glossy glaze that develops a fine crackle pattern. It works well on stoneware and porcelain bodies and produces crackle patterns at midrange temperatures. Turn an orange-brown and pool white in texture when thick. The glaze does not crackle.

glazes.name: Celadon Bloom
glazes.description: Celadon Bloom is a turquoise semi-transparent gloss glaze that contains white crystals and creates an ancient glaze effect. For a lighter crystal effect, two coats of Turquoise SW-201 followed by one coat of Celadon Bloom SW-150 may be applied.
glazes.originalDescription: Celadon Bloom is a turquoise semi-transparent gloss glaze that contains white crystals. The color lightens and crystals remain visible in Cone 10 reduction. For a lighter crystal effect, two coats of Turquoise SW-201 with a third coat of Celadon Bloom SW-150 may be applied.

glazes.name: Olive Float
glazes.description: Olive Float is a dark green gloss glaze with light green crystals, mimicking crystalline glazes and featuring red color changes. For a lighter crystal effect, apply two coats of SW-210 Emerald followed by one coat of SW-151 Olive Float.
glazes.originalDescription: Olive Float is a dark green gloss glaze that contains a light green crystal. It mimics crystalline glazes and features red color changes at Cone 10 reduction temperature. Crystals remain. For a lighter crystal effect, apply two coats of SW-210 Emerald followed by one coat of SW-151 Olive Float.

glazes.name: Blue Splatterware
glazes.description: Blue Splatterware is a white, semi-matte glaze that contains a blue crystal. It creates small blue blooms as the crystal melts, resembling 19th century enamelware. Apply two coats of SW-141 White Matte followed by one coat for a lighter crystal effect.
glazes.originalDescription: Blue Splatterware is a white, semi-matte glaze that contains a blue crystal. As the crystal melts, it creates small blue blooms, resembling 19th century enamelware. For a lighter crystal effect, apply two coats of SW-141 White Matte followed by one coat of SW-152 Blue Splatterware.

glazes.name: Indigo Rain
glazes.description: Indigo Rain is a matte white glaze that contains two-toned purple and violet crystals, which melt to reveal a purple crystal with violet specks. The glaze has a soft subtle beauty.
glazes.originalDescription: Cone 6:Indigo Rain is a matte white glaze that contains a two-toned purple and violet crystal. As the crystal melts, a purple crystal with violet specks appears. The glaze has a soft subtle beauty. Cone 10:Color darkens to variegated light gray/purple. Crystals melt.

glazes.name: Shipwreck
glazes.description: Shipwreck is an aquamarine gloss glaze with opaque opacity and a mysterious appearance reminiscent of underwater footage due to the presence of a small iron speck that emerges as the crystal melts.
glazes.originalDescription: Cone 6:Shipwreck is an aquamarine gloss glaze that contains an iron crystal. As the crystal melts, a small iron speck appears. The glaze appears mysterious like underwater footage. Cone 10:Color darkens.

glazes.name: Winter Wood
glazes.description: Winter Wood is a matte white glaze that contains a variety of brown crystals that melt. For a lighter crystal effect, apply two coats of the base glaze SW-106 Alabaster followed by one coat of SW-155 Winter Wood.
glazes.originalDescription: Cone 6 oxidation (larger image):Winter Wood is a matte white glaze that contains a variety of brown crystals that melt.Cone 10 reduction (smaller image):Color changes to a translucent golden brown.TIP:SW-106 Alabaster is the base glaze. For a lighter crystal effect, you may try applying two coats of SW-106 Alabaster with a third coat of SW-155 Winter Wood.

glazes.name: Galaxy
glazes.description: Galaxy is a semi-transparent charcoal colored gloss glaze that contains three-tone white, orange, and blue crystals. As the crystal melts, a brilliant bloom of white, orange, and blue appears. The glaze is reminiscent of a star being born. Crystals and base showcase more blue hues in Cone 10 reduction.
glazes.originalDescription: Cone 6 oxidation (larger image):Galaxy is a semi-transparent charcoal colored gloss glaze that contains a three-tone white, orange, and blue crystal. As the crystal melts, a brilliant bloom of white, orange, and blue appears. The glaze is reminiscent of a star being born.Cone 10 reduction (smaller image):Crystals and base showcase more blue hues. Crystals melt.TIP:SW-209 Charcoal is the base glaze. For a lighter crystal effect, you may try applying two coats of SW-209 Charcoal with a third coat of SW-156 Galaxy.

glazes.name: Lilac Matte
glazes.description: Semi-opaque matte lilac glaze. The glaze will break over surface textures, pooling around them and creating darker areas where it accumulates. Thicker application will produce a more opaque finish, allowing body characteristics to appear through the glaze on dark and speckled clay bodies.
glazes.originalDescription: Semi-opaque matte lilac glaze. The glaze will break over surface textures, pooling around them and creating darker areas where it accumulates. At Cone 10, it becomes translucent and glossy. Thicker application will produce a more opaque finish, allowing body characteristics to appear through the glaze on dark and speckled clay bodies.

glazes.name: Pink Matte
glazes.description: Semi-opaque matte pink, lighter coatings reveal body characteristics on dark and speckled clay bodies. Thicker application becomes soft pink translucent gloss and more opaque.
glazes.originalDescription: Semi-opaque matte pink, lighter coatings show body characteristics, especially on dark and speckled clay bodies. Thicker application becomes soft pink translucent gloss and more opaque.

glazes.name: Satin Patina
glazes.description: Satin Patina is a rich tonal matte green patina glaze. The thicker the application, the more opaque and homogenous the color will be; thinner application allows more of the clay body to show through. It is a stable glaze.
glazes.originalDescription: Cone 6:Satin Patina is a rich tonal matte green patina glaze. The variation of the glaze is dependent on application. The thicker the application the more opaque and homogenous the color will be, and thinner application will allow more of the clay body to show through the glaze. Satin Patina is a stable glaze.Cone 10:Finish flattens. Color lightens. Brown/orange undertones come through.Note:Not recommended for dinnerware due to surface durability.

glazes.name: Lavender Mist
glazes.description: Cone 6 Lavender Mist is a semi-opaque gloss glaze displaying shades of lavender and brown. The variation depends on application, with thicker layers resulting in a more opaque and uniform color. Cone 10 Color darkens.
glazes.originalDescription: Cone 6:Lavender Mist is a semi-opaque gloss glaze that displays shades of lavender and brown. The variation of the glaze is dependent on application; Thicker application will result in a more opaque and homogenous color.Cone 10:Color darkens.

glazes.name: Norse Blue
glazes.description: Norse Blue is a semi-opaque blue gloss glaze that displays shades of light blue and green. The glaze will break translucent where thinner and pool darker around surface textures. Color may darken in reduction firing.
glazes.originalDescription: Cone 6oxidation (larger image):Norse Blue is a semi-opaque blue gloss glaze that displays shades of light blue and green. The variation of glaze depends on application. The thicker the application, the more homogenous the color will be. The glaze will break translucent where thinner and pool darker around surface textures.Cone 10reduction (smaller image):Color darkens.

glazes.name: Sand & Sea
glazes.description: Sand & Sea is a semi-opaque gloss glaze that displays shades of light green and gold. The glaze will break translucent where thinner and pool darker around surface textures. Color changes to cream/gray/brown at Cone 10 reduction.
glazes.originalDescription: Sand & Sea is a semi-opaque gloss glaze that displays shades of light green and gold. The glaze will break transluscent where thinner and pool darker around surface textures. Color changes to cream/gray/brown at Cone 10 reduction.

glazes.name: Coral Sands
glazes.description: Coral Sands is a semi-opaque gloss glaze that displays shades of orange, yellow, and pink. The glaze will break translucent where thinner and pool darker around surface textures. Color changes to pink/brown at higher temperatures.
glazes.originalDescription: Coral Sands is a semi-opaque gloss glaze that displays shades of orange, yellow, and pink. The glaze will break translucent where thinner and pool darker around surface textures. Color changes to pink/brown at Cone 10 reduction.

glazes.name: Frosted Lemon
glazes.description: Semi-opaque gloss yellow glaze that displays shades of yellow and white. The thicker the application, the more homogenous the color will be. The glaze will break translucent where thinner and pool darker around surface textures. Color can change to light pink/purple at higher temperatures.
glazes.originalDescription: Cone 6:Frosted Lemon is a semi-opaque gloss yellow glaze that displays shades of yellow and white. The variation of glaze depends on application. The thicker the application, the more homogenous the color will be. The glaze will break translucent where thinner and pool darker around surface textures.Cone 10:Color changes to a light pink/purple.

glazes.name: Blue Hydrangea
glazes.description: Blue Hydrangea is a blue floral-inspired glaze featuring yellow and white crystals that melt to reveal large white blooms and smaller yellow blooms.
glazes.originalDescription: Cone 6:Blue Hydrangea is a blue floral-inspired glaze that contains yellow and white crystals. As the crystals melt, large white blooms and smaller yellow blooms appear in the glaze.Cone 10:Color changes to blue. Crystals fade slightly.

glazes.name: Enchanted Forest
glazes.description: Enchanted Forest is a dark charcoal gloss glaze that contains small yellow and medium-sized green crystals. As the crystals melt, a variety of yellow and green blooms appear.
glazes.originalDescription: Cone 6:Enchanted Forest is a dark charcoal gloss glaze that contains small yellow and medium-sized green crystals. As the crystals melt, a variety of yellow and green blooms appear.Cone 10:Base glaze turns dark blue, yellow crystals turn white and green crystals turn blue.

glazes.name: Macadamia
glazes.description: Creamy vanilla matte glaze with accents of gold, color changing to variegated gray/cream with glossy blue becoming more prominent. Glaze breaks over texture and pools glossy blue.
glazes.originalDescription: Macadamia is a creamy vanilla matte glaze that adds accents of gold. Color changes to a variegated gray/cream with glossy blue becoming more prominent. The glaze breaks over texture and can pool glossy blue.

glazes.name: Amber Quartz
glazes.description: Amber Quartz is a warm amber matte glaze that adds accents of tan and brown. The glaze will break translucent where thinner and pool darker around surface textures. It features glossy purple-blue highlights.
glazes.originalDescription: Amber Quartz is a warm amber matte glaze that adds accents of tan and brown. The glaze will break translucent where thinner and pool darker around surface textures. Glossy purple/blue is more prominent.

glazes.name: Leather
glazes.description: Leather is a matte brown glaze that displays shades of blue and green. The glaze breaks translucent where thinner and pools darker around surface textures. Glossy blue becomes more prominent.
glazes.originalDescription: Leather is a matte brown glaze that displays shades of blue and green. The variation of the glaze is dependent on application. The thicker the application, the more homogenous the color will be. The glaze will break translucent where thinner and pool darker around surface textures. Glossy blue becomes more prominent.

glazes.name: Rusted Iron
glazes.description: Semi-matte, opaque glaze displaying shades of metallic orange and brown. Thicker application results in metallic matte characteristics; thinner application leads to a smoother surface and darker color. Increased orange/copper mottling and movement with thicker coats. Take care when using on vertical pieces—reduce glaze thickness towards the bottom third to minimize run-off. Over application can cause pinholing or running.
glazes.originalDescription: Cone 6:Rusted Iron is a semi-matte, opaque glaze that displays shades of metallic orange and brown. The variation of the glaze is dependent on application.Thicker application results in metallic matte characteristics. Thinner application results in a smoother surface and darker color.Cone 10:Increased orange/copper mottling. Increased movement.TIP:This glaze WILL move. Take care when using on a vertical piece – reduce glaze thickness towards the bottom third to minimize run-off. Over application can cause pinholing or running.Wood and soda firing results will vary.

glazes.name: Sandstone
glazes.description: Sandstone is an off-white glaze with brown crystals that melt during the firing. Thicker application and heavy crystal concentration produce more glossy blue/purple variation. Color darkens, and glossy blue/purples become more prominent.
glazes.originalDescription: Cone 6: Sandstone is an off-white glaze with brown crystals that melt during the firing. Thicker application and heavy crystal concentration produce more glossy blue/purple variation. Cone 10: Color darkens, and glossy blue/purples become more prominent.

glazes.name: Raspberry Mist
glazes.description: Raspberry Mist is a gloss, semi-opaque glaze that displays shades of dark and light pink. The thicker the application, the more homogenous the color will be. The glaze will break translucent and appear brighter with thinner application. Color darkens to a deep red at Cone 10 reduction. Brighter color and less variation at Cone 5.
glazes.originalDescription: Raspberry Mist is a gloss, semi-opaque glaze that displays shades of dark and light pink. The variation of glaze depends on application. The thicker the application, the more homogenous the color will be. The glaze will break translucent and appear brighter with thinner application. Color darkens to a deep red at Cone 10 reduction. Brighter color and less variation at Cone 5.

glazes.name: Fool’s Gold
glazes.description: Rich brown base with tiny gold metallic flecks. Breaks translucent and pools darker around surface textures. Color darkens and produces metallic mottling.
glazes.originalDescription: Fool’s Gold is a glossy, semi-transparent glaze consisting of a rich brown base with tiny, gold metallic flecks. The glaze breaks translucent and pools darker around surface textures. Color darkens and produces metallic mottling.

glazes.name: Muddy Waters
glazes.description: Matte brown glaze with crystals melting into a glossy blue. Glossy blue becomes more prominent with increased movement.
glazes.originalDescription: Matte brown glaze with crystals melting into a glossy blue. Heavier crystal application produces more color variation. Glossy blue becomes more prominent with increased movement.

glazes.name: Desert Dusk
glazes.description: Desert Dusk is a warm amber matte glaze with crystals that melt into a glossy purple and blue. Heavier crystal application will produce more color variation. Glossy purple/blue is more prominent.
glazes.originalDescription: Cone 6: Desert Dusk is a warm amber matte glaze with crystals that melt into a glossy purple and blue. Heavier crystal application will produce more color variation. Cone 10: Glossy purple/blue is more prominent. Increased movement.

glazes.name: Night Moth
glazes.description: An opaque gloss glaze with crystals that bloom into sage and gray. For a lighter crystal effect, apply two coats of SW-508 Black Gloss followed by one coat of SW-181 Night Moth.
glazes.originalDescription: Cone 6:Night Moth is an opaque gloss glaze with crystals that bloom into sage and gray.Cone 10:No change.TIP:SW-508 Black Gloss is the base glaze. For a lighter crystal effect, you may try applying two coats of SW-508 Black Gloss with a third coat of SW-181 Night Moth.

glazes.name: Antique Brass
glazes.description: Antique Brass is a soft matte, metallic brass glaze that breaks charcoal.
glazes.originalDescription: Cone 6oxidation (larger image):Antique Brass is a soft matte, metallic brass glaze that breaks charcoal.Cone 10reduction (smaller image):Brass finish becomes brighter and more metallic. Increased movement.Note:Not recommended for dinnerware due to surface durability.

glazes.name: Oxblood
glazes.description: Oxblood is a glossy, transparent glaze that pools red. Heavier application produces more red. Gloss finish is enhanced and less milky in Cone 10 reduction firings with brighter red pooling. Must be applied thick for red variation to appear. Wood and soda firing results will vary.
glazes.originalDescription: Oxblood is a glossy, transparent glaze that pools red. Heavier application produces more red. Gloss finish is enhanced and less milky in Cone 10 reduction firings, with brighter red pooling. Must be applied thick for red variation to appear. Wood and soda firing results will vary.

glazes.name: Speckled Toad
glazes.description: Speckled Toad is a shimmery green with dark grey specks that bloom when fired. The glaze breaks to a semi-transparent olive green.
glazes.originalDescription: Cone 6 oxidation (larger image):Speckled Toad is a shimmery green with dark grey specks that bloom when fired. Glaze breaks a semi-transparant olive green.Cone 10 reduction (smaller image):Color changes to olive green/metallic gray that breaks blue.

glazes.name: Rainforest
glazes.description: Rainforest is a soft matte, emerald green glaze that pools dark and becomes glossy, breaking into a light brown with green variations where it pools. The color changes to light brown with green undertones where thicker application occurs. Increased movement is observed.
glazes.originalDescription: Rainforest is a soft matte, emerald green glaze that will pool dark and glossy and break a light brown. Color changes to light brown with green variation where thick/pooling. Increased movement.

glazes.name: Azurite
glazes.description: Azurite is a soft matte, cobalt blue glaze that will pool dark and glossy and break a light brown. Color changes to green where thin/breaking and remains dark blue where thick/pooling. Increased movement. This glaze can be very mobile; recede coats to avoid run off on vertical pieces and practice caution in combination.
glazes.originalDescription: Cone 6 oxidation (larger image):Azurite is a soft matte, cobalt blue glaze that will pool dark and glossy and break a light brown.Cone 10 reduction (smaller image):Color changes to green where thin/breaking and remains dark blue where thick/pooling. Increased movement.Tip:This glaze can be very mobile; Recede coats to avoid run off on vertical pieces and practice caution in combination.Note:Not recommended for dinnerware due to surface durability.Wood and soda firing results will vary.

glazes.name: Himalayan Salt
glazes.description: Cone 6:Himalayan Salt is a soft matte glaze with light pink and burnt orange mottling that will pool a glossy clear.Cone 10:Color changes to an opaque, variegated gray/white that breaks gray over texture and can pool a glossy purple/blue when thick.Tip:This glaze can be very mobile; Recede coats to avoid run off on vertical pieces and practice caution in combination.Note:Not recommended for dinnerware due to surface durability.Wood and soda firing results will vary.

glazes.name: Cenote
glazes.description: Semi-opaque with sky blue gloss and brown crystals.
glazes.originalDescription: Ancient glazes inspired, semi-opaque with sky blue gloss and brown crystals. Opaque.

glazes.name: Passion Flower
glazes.description: Passionflower Purple is a glossy, opaque deep purple glaze with crystals in varying sizes that bloom into shades of blue, green, and yellow. Crystals soften and lighten, creating a semi-opaque variegated blue that breaks over texture.
glazes.originalDescription: Passionflower Purple is a glossy, opaque, deep purple glaze with crystals in varying sizes that bloom into shades of blue, green, and yellow. The glaze shifts to a semi-opaque variegated blue that breaks over texture. Crystals soften and lighten.

glazes.name: Peppered Plum
glazes.description: Glossy, opaque burgundy glaze with warm taupe float that intensifies with thickness; small opaque black crystals and large semi-transparent burgundy crystals featured. Large crystal shifts to opaque red with light application.
glazes.originalDescription: Peppered Plum is a glossy, opaque, burgundy glaze that breaks over texture and develops a warm taupe float; small opaque black crystals are featured with large semi-transparent burgundy crystals. Large crystal shifts to opaque red with light application. Warm taupe float develops and intensifies with glaze thickness.

glazes.name: Amaryllis
glazes.description: Amaryllis is a glossy, transparent, coral glaze with crystals in varying sizes that bloom into shades of brown, yellow, and orange. Crystals soften and remain stable with heavy application.
glazes.originalDescription: Amaryllis is a glossy, transparent, coral glaze with crystals in varying sizes that bloom into shades of brown, yellow, and orange. Glaze fades to a transparent pink in Cone 10 reduction firing. Crystals soften and remain stable with heavy application.

glazes.name: Ivy
glazes.description: Ivy is a glossy, opaque, variegated green glaze with an iridescent sheen. Green micro-crystallization may develop and break purple over texture. Micro-crystallization enhances.
glazes.originalDescription: Ivy is a glossy, opaque, variegated green glaze with an iridescent sheen. Green micro-crystallization may develop and breaks purple over texture. Micro-crystallization enhances.

glazes.name: Nimbus
glazes.description: Nimbus is a glossy, opaque glaze with a variegated blue appearance that breaks over texture and has an iridescent sheen. Blue micro-crystallization may develop, brightening the glaze to a variegated cyan that shows green/brown over the texture.
glazes.originalDescription: Nimbus is a glossy, opaque, variegated blue glaze that breaks over texture and has an iridescent sheen. Blue micro-crystalization may develop. Cone 10 reduction (small photo): Glaze brightens to a variegated cyan that breaks green/brown over texture.

glazes.name: Riptide
glazes.description: Glaze shifts to grey with white/turquoise variegation, breaking over texture and developing a glossy finish where thick or pooling.
glazes.originalDescription: Glaze shifts grey with white/turquoise variegation, breaking over texture and developing a glossy finish where thick or pooling.

glazes.name: Sand Dollar
glazes.description: Glossy, variegated lavender purple with white micro-crystallization in Cone 10 reduction. Satin, opaque, cream-colored with white and gold mottling in Cone 6 oxidation. Breaks blue/grey over texture. Breaks glossy transparent over texture.
glazes.originalDescription: Sand Dollar shifts to a glossy, variegated lavender purple with white micro-crystallization in Cone 10 reduction. Breaks blue/grey over texture. In Cone 6 oxidation, it is a satin, opaque, cream-colored glaze with white and gold mottling. Breaks glossy transparent over texture.

glazes.name: Fossil Rock
glazes.description: Geologically inspired with mottled earth tones, Fossil Rock is an opaque, tan, matte glaze that breaks over texture. The glaze shifts to ivory with soft gray and warm brown marbling, breaking slate gray over texture.
glazes.originalDescription: Geologically inspired with mottled earth tones, Fossil Rock is an opaque, tan, matte glaze that breaks over texture. Glaze shifts ivory with soft gray and warm brown marbling, breaking slate gray over texture.

glazes.name: Rose Quartz
glazes.description: Glossy, opaque, variegated pink glaze that breaks over texture. Pastel pink micro-crystallization may develop on vertical surfaces. Darkens and desaturates to a dusty rose with red and mauve variegation and white micro-crystallization. Breaks mauve grey over texture. Micro-crystallization effect is enhanced in a rapidly cooling kiln.
glazes.originalDescription: Rose quartz is a glossy, opaque, variegated pink glaze that breaks over texture. Pastel pink micro-crystallization may develop on vertical surfaces. Glaze darkens and desaturates to a dusty rose with red and mauve variegation and white micro-crystallization. Breaks mauve grey over texture. The micro-crystallization effect is enhanced in a rapidly cooling kiln.

glazes.name: Purple Aster
glazes.description: Purple Aster is a glossy, semi-opaque lavender glaze that breaks over texture and develops a pale violet frost; deep blue crystals melt into cascading blossoms of indigo. Crystals can create movement.
glazes.originalDescription: Purple Aster is a glossy, semi-opaque lavender glaze that breaks over texture and develops a pale violet frost; deep blue crystals melt into cascading blossoms of indigo. Cone 10: Color darkens slightly. Crystals can create movement.

glazes.name: Turquoise
glazes.description: Translucent, bluish-green gloss color. One coat adds a faint color gloss, deepening with additional coats to enhance opacity while maintaining some translucency.
glazes.originalDescription: Translucent, bluish-green gloss color. One coat allows clay body characteristics to appear through the glaze, adding a faint color gloss to the surface. Subsequent coats deepen the turquoise color but maintain a translucent fired surface. Three or more coats produce more opacity and less translucency.

glazes.name: Root Beer
glazes.description: Translucent, rich, brown gloss color. One coat allows a faint color gloss to the surface; deeper color with two to three coats, remaining translucent even at multiple coats. Three plus coats produce more opacity and less translucency.
glazes.originalDescription: Translucent, rich, brown gloss color. One coat allows a faint color gloss to the surface; deeper color with two to three coats, remaining translucent even at multiple coats. Three plus coats produce more opacity and less translucency. Cone 10: Color darkens with additional coats.

glazes.name: Amber Topaz
glazes.description: Amber Topaz provides a light honey-blonde color, becoming deeper with additional coats while remaining translucent. One coat allows clay body characteristics to appear through, adding a faint gloss; three coats or more produce more opacity and less translucency. Heavy application may increase the incidence of crazing.
glazes.originalDescription: Cone 6:Amber Topaz provides a translucent, light honey-blonde color. glazes.description: Iridescent micro-crystallization in shades of lilac and pale yellow will develop during the firing process. Glaze darkens to blue, purple and white variegation. Micro-Pearl achieves a stable, semi-opaque, frosted white result with a pearlescent satin finish when fired to cone 5.

glazes.name: Micro Champagne
glazes.description: Micro Champagne is a glossy, translucent amber glaze with a mauve float that breaks over texture. It blooms into shimmering gold micro-crystallization when fired, resulting in a stable, semi-opaque, golden-amber color with a pearlescent satin finish.

glazes.name: Micro Jade

glazes.description: Micro Cerulean is a glossy, translucent, medium blue glaze with an aqua float that breaks over texture. It blooms into shimmering blue micro-crystallization during firing. When fired to cone 5, it achieves a stable, semi-opaque, deep blue result with a pearlescent satin finish.

glazes.description: Opal Lustre is a glossy, semi-opaque glaze that displays variegated shades of blues, greens, and purples. Breaks warm brown over texture. Results may vary based on clay body, application thickness, firing process, and temperature.

glazes.description: Satin, opaque, mottled brown glaze that breaks over texture. Contains glossy brown crystals. Base color is stable, crystals can create movement. Color becomes darker metallic with more flowing crystals at Cone 10 reduction. Can be used in oxidation or reduction firings.
glazes.originalDescription: Black Timber is a satin, opaque, mottled brown glaze that breaks over texture. Contains glossy brown crystals. Base color is stable, crystals can create movement. Can be used in oxidation or reduction firings.

glazes.name: White Opal
glazes.description: White Opal forms a clear- to- milky white gloss surface. One coat produces the clearest finish; subsequent layering increases the incidence of a white precipitate forming. Prominent white to bluish-white colors will emerge where the glaze pools and gathers around surface texture. Thinner application will provide a glossy transparent finish. Thicker application will provide white floating. White coloration is more visible on darker clay bodies (Buff, Manganese Speck).

Thinner application will provide a glossy transparent finish; thicker application will provide light pink floating. Surface decoration will be visible under the glaze.

glazes.name: White Melt Gloop
glazes.description: Melt Gloop is a stoneware glaze designed for application in dots or creating a dripping effect down your ceramic piece. Increased movement and texture are achieved by applying glaze with a syringe or squeeze bottle on greenware or soft-fired, cone 04 stoneware bisque (1940°F/1060°C). For a more dynamic effect and a dripping appearance, apply larger dots on a vertical surface. The glaze can flow over textured areas. For better adhesion, dampen the bisque with a sponge before applying. Heavy application or over-handling may cause the glaze to pop off. Handle with care. Can be layered and marbled with other Melt Gloop colors.
